response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack software engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and some systems-level work (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ tooling, embedded robotics), but the role demands deep hardware-oriented expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. The performance work described is application/service-level optimization, not silicon or accelerator architecture, making this a significant domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of software experience with a Bachelor's degree, exceeding the years-of-experience bar
Genuine systems experience: low-latency async I/O services in DLang handling millions of requests/sec
Some hardware-adjacent exposure (robotic arms, C/C++ measurement tooling, audio hardware thesis project)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD experience anywhere in the resume
No deep-learning workload, DNN training/inference, or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/accelerator or hardware co-design experience
Solid low-level performance and systems programming foundation
